text: The surgeon uses the da Vinci robotic system with three arms to perform a cholecystectomy. The fundus of the gallbladder is retracted using a Stryker Mini Alligator, while two bipolar Maryland forceps are employed for dissection.
The surgeon has separated the peritoneum around the cystic particle, preparing to create a posterior window for further dissection.

The surgeon separates the cystic duct from the cystic artery, ensuring that the peritoneum is separated up to the body. Care is taken to avoid tenting of the common bile duct during the robotic procedure.

The surgeon identifies the anatomical structures within Calot's triangle, with the cystic duct positioned medially and the cystic artery laterally. The common bile duct (CBD) is visible medially, and the inferior edge of the liver is exposed. A window is formed, and two hem-o-lok clips are applied to the cystic duct for secure ligation.
The surgeon applies bipolar cautery to the cystic artery and uses scissors to cut it without applying a clip. The gallbladder is gradually separated from the liver bed.
